title: Systemic PRNG call-count phase desync (rng_seed) — residual combat divergence after sim-tick alignment: guards perceive/act a few ticks off retail
repro: dam_combat_guard6 both-sides under --align tick (FID-0062): after removing the alignment artifact, residual guard divergence (315 actiontype) is driven by native vs retail PRNG randomGetNext() being called a different NUMBER of times per tick (call-count phase), so probabilistic see-target (randomGetNext()%distance==0) fires a few ticks off — e.g. guard6 perceives +108t native vs +76t stock. The PRNG itself is bit-faithful (random.c:279); the CALL COUNT diverges.

history.note: Filed from FID-0062 re-validation. THE real systemic owner of the residual perception-timing divergence that survives correct sim-tick alignment. NOT a guard-AI bug (perception logic is faithful ASM). FID-0054 = mostly artifact + THIS. FID-0011/0012/0013 must be re-captured under --align tick; whatever survives is THIS class (RNG call-count phase + tiny position drift), not chr.c/stan.c logic. Root-cause needs: find where native makes a different number of randomGetNext() calls per tick than retail (extra/missing consumers, order, or a call gated differently). XL systemic. Related: rng_seed §5/§8, FID-0046 (Streets nondeterminism).
